saints.name: Saint Theodore the Sykeote, Bishop of Anastasiopolis

troparion.text: You were sanctified from your earliest childhood  /  and were shown to be filled with grace.  / You illumined the world with miracles  / and drove away a multitude of demons, O Holy Hierarch Theodore.  /  Therefore, entreat the Lord on our behalf.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben “Today the Virgin...”)
As upon a fiery chariot, you ascended on the virtues to the heavenly mansions, O God-bearer Theodore.  /  You were an Angel living on earth with men;  / and a man dancing with joy with the holy Angels. / Therefore,  O Theodore, /  you proved to be a divine vessel of miracles.

troparion.text: Holy Apostle Nathaniel, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

saints.name: Apostle and Evangelist Luke of the Seventy

troparion.text: Holy Apostle Luke, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

saints.name: Holy Apostles of the 70 Apelles, Luke (Loukios), and Clement

troparion.text: Holy Apostles Apelles, Luke, and Clement, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Vitalius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Savva, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

saints.name: Hieromartyr Platon the Newmartyr of Banjaluka

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Platon, entreat Christ God to save our souls.
